Antonycc claims Issue #8 "[chunk 2/3] Cron matching + next-run computation" has been closed on polycode-public/sandbox, with the parser delivered on `main`. Issue #10 "[chunk 3/3] Cron edge cases: month-end skip + leap year" has been closed, focusing on handling month-end skips and leap years. This slice ensures correct next-run/matching for edge cases, finalizes documentation and tests, and includes acceptance criteria for specific tests for month-end and leap year cases, ensuring no regression in existing functionality and comprehensive README updates.

Antonycc (trust: unknown) claims that a visitor pushed to the main branch of the polycode-public/sandbox repository on 2026-06-14 with commits 5bc5a9d5 and 9bf02677, resetting the benchmark and fixing issue #5. Another visitor pushed with commit 9fd94dc0 on 2026-06-17, delivering intent and fixing issue #13, touching README.md, src/lib/main.js, and tests/unit/main.test.js. github-actions[bot] pushed with commit 9fd94dc0 on 2026-06-17, touching the same files. Antony Cartwright pushed to main with commit 5521a314 on 2026-06-16 to grant id-token for the M5-token workflow

Antonycc claims that issues #5 and #8, related to cron parser, serializer, and validation, as well as cron matching and next-run computation, are closed and implemented in `src/lib/main.js`. Issue #10, concerning cron edge cases for month-end skip and leap year, has also been closed. This PR focuses on correcting next-run and matching for calendar edge cases, finalizing documentation, and ensuring sufficient search windows in `nextRuns`. Tests and the README will be updated to reflect these changes. Additionally, issue #11 has been addressed, with the month-end next-run iteration corrected to avoid an overshoot in the iterator.

antonycc (trust: unknown) claims: Issue #15 for the dense-encoding implementation on repository polycode-public/sandbox has been closed. The second chunk added a third encoding denser than base85 and a `listEncodings` function. The scope involved ensuring the third encoding's round-trip functionality and metadata accuracy for registered encodings. A fix was made for `listEncodings` metadata tests to ensure consistent charset size and correct bitsPerChar calculations.
